description_long: This is a rectangular, vintage-style record player with a dark, matte-finished body and a visibly worn, circular turntable platter at its center. The platter is heavily scratched, suggesting frequent use, and is surrounded by a slightly raised edge. A straight, metallic tonearm with a blocky counterweight and a stylus assembly is mounted on a circular base at one corner, extending over the platter. Two small, square control buttons and a long, rectangular slider are positioned on the top surface, likely for power and speed adjustment. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, and the materials appear to be primarily metal and plastic. The record player lacks a dust cover, exposing all its functional components.
description: A dark, rectangular record player with a scratched platter, metallic tonearm, and simple control buttons, lacking a dust cover. The design is utilitarian and shows signs of heavy use.
description_view_0: The record player is seen from a front-left angle, highlighting the scratched platter, tonearm base, and two square control buttons.
description_view_1: Viewed from the front-right, the tonearm and its counterweight are prominent, along with the long slider control and the platter's worn surface.
description_view_2: From the rear-right, the tonearm's full length and its position over the platter are visible, as well as the slider and the edge of the platter.
description_view_3: The rear-left view shows the tonearm's mounting base, the platter, and the absence of a dust cover, with minimal controls visible.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and centrally located, the platter is circular, black, and heavily scratched, occupying a significant portion of the device's top surface. It appears to be made of metal or hard plastic with a matte, worn finish and no visible grooves or decorative patterns.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, straight, and metallic with a light gray finish, mounted on a circular base at one corner. It is of medium length, extending over the platter, and features a blocky counterweight at the rear. The tonearm is resting in its holder, not raised.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is present as a small, distinct component at the end of the tonearm, appearing as a tiny protrusion, likely made of metal or plastic. It is attached to the tonearm and visually separate from the main arm.
attribute_control_knobs: Two square control buttons are visible on the top left, made of plastic and colored gray, along with a long, rectangular slider on the right side. No visible labels or markings are present on the controls.
